meta.title: Eco Parque West View | Natural Eco Park in San Andrés, Colombia
meta.description: Explore Eco Parque West View, a top snorkeling destination in San Andrés, Colombia. Enjoy Caribbean underwater ecology and diving fun.

hero.title: Eco Parque West View
hero.subtitle: Top Snorkeling Destination in San Andrés

intro.title: Explore Eco Parque West View
intro.description: Discover the top natural eco park on San Andrés Island, Colombia — a perfect vacation destination blending Caribbean underwater ecology with natural adventure. Unlike traditional artificial water parks, West View is a completely natural cove. It features crystal-clear waters perfect for snorkeling, an iconic wooden slide straight into the sea, a diving trampoline, and a hidden underwater Poseidon statue.

visitGuide.title: Visiting Tips
items: 🌞 Morning Tour: Arrive early to avoid crowds. Rent a snorkeling mask to explore the clear underwater world and the Poseidon statue.
🌴 Midday Activities: Sunbathe on the rocky coastal rest area and enjoy fresh coconut water.
🌊 Afternoon Time: Experience the sea slide and diving trampoline, then enjoy the stunning Caribbean sunset before heading back.

alsoKnownAs.title: Core Highlights
items: 🐠 Top Snorkeling Experience: Features crystal-clear waters where you can swim alongside schools of colorful tropical fish as soon as you enter.
🌊 Natural Sea Slide & Trampoline: Experience the thrill of sliding directly into the Caribbean Sea, or jump from the dedicated diving platform.
🔱 Underwater Poseidon Statue: Dive into the water to discover the hidden Poseidon statue, a must-see underwater photo spot.
🥥 Eco & Nature Integration: Preserves the original rocky coastal landscape while offering basic services like lounge chairs and a snack bar.

route.title: 💡 Pro Tips & Itinerary
steps: Morning: Arrive at the park, rent a snorkeling mask and life jacket (or bring your own), and enjoy the clear underwater world and Poseidon statue while it's less crowded.
Midday: Relax and sunbathe on the rocky coastal area, and taste the local coconut water or snacks sold in the park.
Afternoon: Experience the thrilling sea slide and diving trampoline, and capture the exciting moments of jumping into the Caribbean Sea with friends.
supplements: To protect the fragile marine ecology and coral reefs, please use reef-safe sunscreen or wear long-sleeved rash guards when entering the water.
When snorkeling in the sea, never touch or step on corals and marine life.
Natural waters are greatly affected by the weather; please check real-time sea conditions before visiting. Freshwater showers are available for rinsing off after swimming.

knowledge.title: Discover the Natural Charm of Eco Parque West View

sections.title: Natural Cove Experience
sections.content: Unlike traditional artificial water parks, West View is a completely natural cove. It preserves the pristine ecological landscape of the Caribbean Sea, allowing visitors to dive directly into the water and snorkel in a real marine environment.

sections.title: Eco-Friendly Facilities
sections.content: The park provides essential facilities like wooden sea slides, diving trampolines, and freshwater showers, seamlessly integrated into the natural rocky coastline.

sections.title: Location & Transportation
sections.content: Eco Parque West View is located on the Circunvalar (ring road) on the west side of San Andrés Island, making it an essential stop for a self-drive island tour.

basicInfo.title: Quick Facts
basicInfo.facilitiesValue: Snorkel rentals, life jackets, natural sea slide, diving board, freshwater showers, beach bar

hours.title: Opening Hours & Visitor Info
hours.winterTime: Bring reef-safe sunscreen, swimwear and plenty of water
hours.warningTime: San Andrés has a hot climate. It is perfect for snorkeling when the sea is calm.
hours.tip: The park offers lockers, showers, and a snack bar.

tickets.title: Tickets & Estimated Prices
tickets.cardPrice: Cash only on-site. Price usually includes a small piece of bread to feed the fish.
tickets.officialNotice: For the latest updates, please refer to the

transport.title: How to Get There
transport.airportDesc: San Andrés has its own airport. You can take a taxi or rent a vehicle from the airport to the attraction.
transport.selfDriveDesc: It is highly recommended to rent a golf cart or scooter and drive along the Circunvalar (ring road). The coastal views are excellent, and there is a parking area outside the park.
transport.busDesc: You can take a local bus and get off at the West View stop on the ring road.
transport.intercityDesc: The most popular way to get around San Andrés Island is renting a golf cart or scooter for a flexible island tour.
transport.cyclingDesc: Visitors staying nearby can also ride a bicycle.
transport.tipsDesc: Plan enough time for your island tour and prepare sunscreen and snorkeling gear before heading out.

historyTimeline.title: Ecology & Development

items.title: Natural Rocky Cove
items.description: Eco Parque West View was originally a completely natural rocky cove on San Andrés Island, famous for its exceptionally clear waters and abundant tropical fish.

items.title: Nature-Integrated Renovation
items.description: To facilitate safe water entry for visitors, locals built rustic wooden slides, diving boards, and handrails along the rocky coast, creating its classic appearance today.

items.title: Marine Conservation & Diving Hub
items.description: Today, it is not only a popular snorkeling destination but also emphasizes marine ecological protection, featuring the iconic underwater Poseidon statue for divers to explore.

historyTimeline.guideContent: It is highly recommended to rent a golf cart and drive along the Circunvalar (ring road). The scenery along the way is spectacular, and parking is available outside the park.

officialManagement.title: Management & Ownership
officialManagement.text: Eco Parque West View and the surrounding marine environment are managed with the support of the San Andrés government and regional environmental agencies (such as CORALINA) to protect this unique natural wonder.
